verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes cytosolic HSP70 (HSP72), canonically a cytoplasmic/ER chaperone. However, multiple lines of evidence document its translocation to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane under stress, oncogenic transformation, and immunogenic/apoptotic cell death. Tumor cells constitutively display membrane HSP70 (mHSP70) on their surface, and this ecto-HSP70 pool has been extensively exploited as a tumor-selective surface target for NK-cell-activating agents, antibody-based diagnostics, and CAR-T approaches. Cell-death and heat-stress conditions also drive PM surface display. This qualifies as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility.
